title: The Boundless: The Oldest Philosophical Fragment
description: The single surviving fragment of Anaximander — the oldest known philosophical text in Western history.
introduction: This single fragment — preserved by Simplicius in the 6th century AD — is the oldest surviving sentence of Western philosophy. Anaximander of Miletus proposed that the origin of all things is the apeiron (the Boundless), an infinite, indefinite substance from which all determinate things emerge and to which they return. His vision of cosmic justice — things paying recompense for their existence — anticipates both Heraclitean flux and the modern concept of entropy.

translation.original_text: ἐξ ὧν δὲ ἡ γένεσίς ἐστι τοῖς οὖσι, καὶ τὴν φθορὰν εἰς ταῦτα γίνεσθαι κατὰ τὸ χρεών· διδόναι γὰρ αὐτὰ δίκην καὶ τίσιν ἀλλήλοις τῆς ἀδικίας κατὰ τὴν τοῦ χρόνου τάξιν.
translation.text: From what things existing objects come to be, into those same things does their destruction take place, according to what must be; for they pay penalty and recompense to each other for their injustice according to the ordering of time.
